title: 2026: 10 Free Zoom Alternatives - Video Tools Complete Review
description: Comprehensive review of Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Webex, Jitsi, and 10 free video tools. Compare features and pricing to find the best free video confe.
content:
10 Free Zoom Alternatives: 2026 Video Conferencing Guide

Overview

This comprehensive guide reviews the top free alternatives to Zoom for video conferencing in 2026. We compare Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Webex, Jitsi Meet, and other free platforms, analyzing meeting limits, participant caps, features, and pricing to help remote teams find cost-effective video solutions.

Why Seek Zoom Alternatives

Teams consider alternatives to Zoom for various reasons:

  • Cost savings: Zoom's 40-minute free meeting limit
  • Platform integration: Tools that integrate with existing software
  • Data privacy: Open-source and self-hosted options
  • Feature requirements: Specific needs like whiteboarding or breakout rooms

Google Meet Free

Google Meet offers accessible video meetings:

  • Meeting duration: 60 minutes for free personal accounts
  • Participants: Up to 100 participants free
  • Google integration: Seamless with Gmail and Google Calendar
  • Screen sharing: Built-in screen and window sharing
  • Pricing: Free with Google account, $6/user/month for Business Starter

Microsoft Teams Free

Microsoft Teams provides comprehensive meetings:

  • Meeting duration: 60 minutes for free tier
  • Participants: Up to 100 participants
  • Recording: Cloud recording in paid plans
  • Office integration: Direct Word and PowerPoint sharing
  • Pricing: Free tier available, $6/user/month for Business Basic

Webex Free

Webex offers generous free features:

  • Meeting duration: Up to 50 minutes free
  • Participants: 100 participants in free meetings
  • Whiteboarding: Built-in collaborative whiteboard
  • Recording: Local recording available
  • Pricing: Free tier, $15/user/month for Starter

Jitsi Meet Open Source

Jitsi provides unlimited free meetings:

  • No meeting limits: Unlimited duration and participants
  • Self-hosting: Deploy on your own server
  • Privacy-focused: Open source with full transparency
  • Features: Screen sharing, recording, and live streaming
  • Pricing: Completely free, hosting costs apply for self-deployment

Additional Free Alternatives

Other notable free video tools include:

  • Skype: 4-hour free meetings, up to 100 participants
  • Discord: Unlimited voice/video in servers
  • Signal: Encrypted video calls for privacy focus
  • Facebook Messenger: 50 participants, easy access
  • WhatsApp: 32 participants, mobile-focused

Feature Comparison Summary

Key comparison points across platforms:

  • Meeting length: Jitsi unlimited, others 40-60 minutes
  • Participants: Range from 32 to 100 free
  • Integration: Google Meet and Teams integrate with productivity suites
  • Privacy: Jitsi and Signal offer strongest privacy controls

Selection Recommendations

Choose based on specific needs:

  • Google users: Google Meet for seamless integration
  • Microsoft ecosystem: Microsoft Teams for Office integration
  • Long meetings: Jitsi or Skype for unlimited duration
  • Privacy priority: Jitsi or Signal for data control
  • Large groups: Webex or Teams for 100 participants
